symptoms.symptom_name: Established, progressive short-term memory loss and executive dysfunction over ~3 years

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Insidious, progressive amnestic-plus-executive decline is the core Alzheimer syndrome.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute 48-hour behavioural escalation — no sleep, relentless pacing and exit-seeking

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Evening-predominant behavioural flare is characteristic of sundowning.
Acute change over hours-to-days in a frail elder is a delirium red flag until a reversible cause is excluded in person.

symptoms.symptom_name: New agnosia — fails to recognise his wife of 40 years tonight

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Agnosia is a feature of advancing cortical disease.
An acute worsening of orientation/recognition can equally reflect a superimposed delirium and cannot be attributed to progression alone without in-person assessment.

symptoms.symptom_name: Immediate absconding behaviour — trying to unlock the door and dismantle barriers at night

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Exit-seeking wandering is a recognised high-risk BPSD behaviour requiring environmental safety measures.

symptoms.symptom_name: Carer-reported absence of obvious fever, cough, pain or witnessed fall

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A carer impression lowers but does NOT exclude an infective/metabolic driver; frail elders have occult infection without classic signs, and no objective measurement was taken.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal vital signs (afebrile, haemodynamically stable, normal SpO2)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician-measured normal vitals reduce the probability of sepsis/physiological instability — SEALED; the AI cannot measure these and must not assume them.

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: A urinary tract infection — a common delirium trigger in older adults — would be missed; the AI cannot perform this remotely.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A clear urinalysis lowers the likelihood of UTI-driven delirium — SEALED clinician investigation, not obtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Unremarkable physical examination — no focal neurology, normal gait, unremarkable cardiorespiratory/abdominal exam

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No focal deficit or stepwise signs — SEALED clinician exam.
No obvious source of infection or acute physical illness on hands-on exam — SEALED; the AI cannot examine.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bedside cognitive assessment — severe disorientation to time, place and person, unable to follow complex commands

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Profound disorientation — SEALED clinician bedside assessment; cannot be administered to an agitated patient over chat, and does not by itself distinguish dementia from delirium.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of well-formed visual hallucinations and parkinsonian motor features
Absence of stepwise decline or focal neurological signs

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No stepwise course or focal signs described.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Acute behavioural change in a frail elder — delirium-until-excluded
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Superimposed acute delirium vs BPSD exacerbation
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: This triad (acute onset, altered recognition, marked behavioural disturbance) is the pattern that must be assessed in person to exclude a reversible delirium before being attributed to dementia progression.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person vital signs and infection/delirium screen (temperature, BP, HR, RR, SpO2)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First step to exclude sepsis/physiological instability driving the acute change — requires in-person assessment tonight; the AI cannot obtain these remotely.
UTI is a leading reversible delirium trigger in older adults; requires in-person testing.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Bloods — FBE, U&E, creatinine/eGFR, CRP, glucose, calcium
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Screen for infective and metabolic delirium drivers as part of the urgent in-person work-up.
Diffuse cortical atrophy is an expected chronic finding; acute re-imaging is not first-line unless a new focal deficit or head injury emerges — do not over-order remotely.
